questions.question: In triangle ABC, angle A measures 50° and angle B measures 65°. What is the measure of angle C?
questions.explanation: The sum of angles in a triangle equals 180°. So angle C = 180° − 50° − 65° = 65°.

questions.question: An isosceles triangle has two equal sides and one of the base angles measures 42°. What is the measure of the vertex angle (the angle between the two equal sides)?
questions.explanation: In an isosceles triangle, the two base angles are equal. So both base angles are 42°. The vertex angle = 180° − 42° − 42° = 96°.

questions.question: A right triangle has one angle of 35°. What is the measure of the third angle?
questions.explanation: A right triangle always has one 90° angle. The third angle = 180° − 90° − 35° = 55°.

questions.question: Two angles in a triangle measure 48° and 73°. Is this triangle acute, right, or obtuse?
questions.explanation: The third angle = 180° − 48° − 73° = 59°. Since all three angles (48°, 59°, 73°) are less than 90°, the triangle is acute.

questions.question: In an equilateral triangle, what is the measure of each interior angle?
questions.explanation: An equilateral triangle has three equal angles. Since the sum is 180°, each angle = 180° ÷ 3 = 60°.

questions.question: A triangle has angles in the ratio 2:3:4. What is the measure of the smallest angle?
questions.explanation: Let the angles be 2x, 3x, and 4x. Then 2x + 3x + 4x = 180°, so 9x = 180°, giving x = 20°. The smallest angle is 2x = 40°.
